evals.prompt: Assess the hydraulic adequacy of a bridge crossing on a national road in Uganda. The bridge has 3 spans of 15 m each (45 m total opening) with 3 round-nosed piers each 1.2 m wide. The river is alluvial with a natural channel width of 45 m, mean depth at Q100 of 3.0 m, bed material D50 = 1 mm (medium sand), and channel slope 0.001 m/m. The design flood Q100 = 250 m3/s. The bridge soffit is 5.0 m above the existing bed level. Determine: waterway area adequacy, pier scour depth using HEC-18 CSU equation with appropriate K-factors, contraction scour, total scour depth, freeboard check (1.5 m required for national road), and afflux assessment.
evals.expected_output: Waterway area: 41.4 x 3.0 = 124.2 m2, V = 2.01 m/s (exceeds 1.5 m/s sand limit). Lacey regime width = 76.5 m (bridge constricts to 59% of regime width). Live-bed conditions (V > Vc). Contraction scour approximately 1.5 m. Pier scour (CSU): K1=1.0, K2=1.0, K3=1.1, K4=1.0, ys approximately 2.0-2.5 m. Total scour approximately 4.0-4.5 m. Freeboard = 5.0 - 3.0 = 2.0 m > 1.5 m (PASS). Afflux estimated < 0.5 m. HEC-18 methodology applied per MoWT Vol 2 Section 10.

assertions.text: Pier scour depth calculated using HEC-18 CSU equation with K1, K2, K3, K4 correction factors all stated
Pier scour depth in range 1.5-3.5 m (depends on exact K-factor values and intermediate rounding)
Contraction scour estimated using live-bed equation (V exceeds critical velocity for D50=1mm)
Freeboard check performed: 2.0 m exceeds 1.5 m minimum for national road (MoWT Section 10.3.2f)
HEC-18 methodology referenced (not culvert hydraulics -- Skill #39 is distinct from Skill #31)
Lacey regime width calculated and compared with bridge opening to assess constriction

evals.prompt: Assess the same bridge crossing (45 m opening, 3 piers at 1.2 m, Q100 = 250 m3/s, mean depth 3.0 m, D50 = 1 mm) for a UK context. Apply appropriate UK standards and climate change considerations.
evals.expected_output: UK approach references CIRIA C742 and DMRB BD 97/CD 356. Climate change uplift applied to design flow (typically 20-25%, giving Q100cc approximately 300-312 m3/s). Scour assessment uses HEC-18 methodology (accepted in UK practice) with climate-adjusted flows. Soffit clearance checked against DMRB requirements (typically 600 mm minimum). Knowledge-based flag noted since CIRIA/DMRB documents not available locally for verification.

assertions.text: References CIRIA C742 or DMRB BD 97/CD 356 (not MoWT)
Climate change uplift applied to design flow (increase of 20-40%)
Knowledge-based flag noted for UK module
HEC-18 methodology accepted for UK practice alongside CIRIA methods

evals.prompt: A bridge on a national road in Uganda has a 15 m opening over a river with a natural channel width of 40 m. Q100 = 150 m3/s. The bridge has a single 1.0 m wide pier. The constriction ratio is 15/40 = 0.375. Mean depth at Q100 is 2.5 m, D50 = 0.5 mm (fine sand). Assess the hydraulic adequacy and identify any critical concerns.
evals.expected_output: CRITICAL -- Severe constriction. Constriction ratio 0.375 (bridge opening only 37.5% of natural channel). Lacey regime width = 4.836 x 150^0.5 = 59.2 m -- bridge opening is only 25% of regime width. Very high contraction scour expected. Mean velocity V = 150/(15 x 2.5) = 4.0 m/s -- very high for fine sand. Significant afflux likely exceeding 0.5 m limit. Escalation: widen bridge opening or add relief openings. Upstream flood impact assessment required.

assertions.text: Severe constriction identified (constriction ratio < 0.5 or bridge width << regime width)
High contraction scour warning raised due to narrow opening relative to natural channel
Afflux likely exceeds 0.5 m limit -- escalation for upstream flood impact assessment
Recommendation to widen bridge opening or add relief openings

evals.prompt: Assess the hydraulic adequacy of a proposed bridge over a seasonal river in Kenya. The design flow is 450 m3/s, the waterway width is 30m, and the river bed is sandy gravel. Check freeboard, estimate scour depth, and verify afflux.
evals.expected_output: References RDM 2.2 Ch 5 for bridge hydraulics. Estimates scour depth using Section 5.6 methods (pier scour, contraction scour). Checks freeboard from Table 5.2. Checks afflux against 0.5m limit. Recommends protection if scour depth is significant.

assertions.text: References RDM 2.2 Chapter 5 for bridge hydraulics methodology
Estimates scour depth using Section 5.6 methods (pier scour equation)
Checks freeboard from Table 5.2 or Section 5.3
Recommends scour protection if scour depth is significant
